purpose: Flat deterministic, music-first QC knowledge pack for resolving findings into Ear Lessons, creative-keep guidance, and searchable learning content. This file is runtime-safe, reviewable, and AI-free.

keepPolicy.safe_creative: User can keep intentionally with light confirmation.
keepPolicy.review_recommended: User can keep intentionally after seeing the tradeoff.
keepPolicy.strong_warning: User can keep only after a stronger warning.
keepPolicy.must_fix: User should not be allowed to keep as-is for release.

migration.mergePolicy: Merged by knowledgeId; v3 wins on collision.
migration.safetyPolicy: riskLevel === must_fix forces canKeepIntentional = false at write time.

knowledgeCards.title: Cabinet sounds hollow
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Guitar tone loses body or sounds scooped in a strange way.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Cabinet and mic blending combine filtered paths that must line up.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Multi-mic blends add depth. Misaligned blends cancel tone.
knowledgeCards.fix: Align IR/mic phase or reduce problematic mic blend.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A hollow cab tone can be a special lo-fi or nasal effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use palm mutes, chords, and single-note leads.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hollow cab? It may disappear in a mix.

knowledgeCards.title: Cabinet sounds boxy
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The tone feels trapped in a small cardboard box.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Cabinet resonance and mic position shape midrange body.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Boxiness can feel vintage. Too much makes the tone small.
knowledgeCards.fix: Change mic position, reduce low-mid resonance, or choose a more open cab.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Boxy tone can work for garage, telephone, or old-radio effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use guitar chords, snare, vocals, and midrange synths.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the box? It is a narrow, vintage flavor.

knowledgeCards.title: Amp sag chokes the attack
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Hard hits collapse or feel slow to recover.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Sag models power supply droop under loud playing.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More sag feels spongy and vintage. Less sag feels tight and immediate.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower sag or increase recovery speed.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Sag is a beloved amp feel for blues, rock, and vintage tones.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use palm mutes, big chords, and loud single notes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the sag? It makes the amp feel softer and slower.

knowledgeCards.title: Knob passes but the whole plugin still fails
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The fixed control behaves well alone, but another preset or interaction still fails.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Full-plugin QC catches interactions between presets, controls, and modules.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Focused tests are fast. Full tests prove release safety.
knowledgeCards.fix: Run a full sweep after local fixes and resolve remaining linked findings.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Passing one control does not mean the whole plugin is ready.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Retest all presets after a control-level repair.

knowledgeCards.title: QC warning may be about plugin intent
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: QC flags behavior that may actually be the point of the plugin.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Capability and intent metadata let QC judge the plugin against the right goal.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Strict QC finds problems. Intent-aware QC avoids punishing creative design.
knowledgeCards.fix: Route the finding to capability confirmation before changing sound.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Intent must be confirmed and saved, not guessed.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare the issue against the plugin's stated purpose and target family.

knowledgeCards.title: Whole plugin passes but one knob path fails
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The default preset seems fine, but a specific knob position creates a problem.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Control-scope QC tests one knob or section across its range.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Full-plugin QC checks release readiness. Control QC checks edit safety.
knowledgeCards.fix: Fix or limit the problematic control range and rerun control-scope QC.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A risky knob range can be kept only if labeled and bounded.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep the exact knob while monitoring the flagged issue.

knowledgeCards.title: Fix only works on the test signal
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The issue passes pink noise but still sounds wrong on music.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Verification should use both measurement signals and musical sources.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Pink noise is consistent. Music proves the fix feels right.
knowledgeCards.fix: Retest with family-specific audio examples after the measurement pass.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A fix is not truly trusted until it works musically.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use drums, bass, vocals, full mix, and family-specific stress clips.

knowledgeCards.title: Automation jumps or clicks
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Automated moves click, jump, or sound stepped.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Automation smoothing turns DAW moves into clean audio changes.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast automation follows the user. Smooth automation protects audio quality.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add smoothing or better parameter interpolation.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Clicks from automation should not ship unless a glitch mode is explicit.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Automate every exposed knob quickly and slowly.

knowledgeCards.title: Mono bass setting reaches too high
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Low mids or guitars lose width, not just sub bass.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Mono bass collapses low frequencies to the center.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Mono low end is club-safe. Too high makes the mix narrow.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower the mono-bass crossover or make it preset-specific.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Higher mono crossover can be useful for strict club/mastering tools.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use full mix, bass, guitars, and stereo synths.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the high mono crossover? It will narrow more than the sub.

knowledgeCards.title: Bitcrush destroys detail
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound turns grainy, blocky, or almost unrecognizable.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Bit depth controls how many volume steps the signal can use.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fewer bits create stronger digital grit. More bits preserve detail.
knowledgeCards.fix: Raise bit depth or lower wet mix.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Destroyed detail is the point of many bitcrush effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use drums, bass, synth leads, and vocals.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the crushed detail? It is a digital destruction choice.

knowledgeCards.title: Sample-rate crush aliases too hard
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Digital side tones, whistles, or robotic grit appear around the source.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Downsampling lowers the audio rate and can create alias tones.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More downsampling is crunchy. Less downsampling keeps musical pitch clearer.
knowledgeCards.fix: Raise sample-rate amount, add filtering, or reduce wet mix.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Aliasing is valid for retro game and glitch effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use high notes, cymbals, and vocal consonants.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the aliasing? It should read as digital style.

knowledgeCards.title: Bypass still changes tone
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin still colors the sound when it is supposed to be bypassed.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Bypass should usually let the original sound through unchanged.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Some analog-style plugins intentionally keep transformer or console color active, but users must know that.
knowledgeCards.fix: Make bypass clean, or label the always-on color clearly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Always-on coloration can be intentional for analog vibe, console trim, or tube path emulation.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Toggle bypass and listen for tone change without changing knobs.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep color in bypass? Label it clearly so users know bypass is not fully clean.
mustFixIf: bypass creates delay or phase shift unexpectedly

knowledgeCards.title: Dry path is intentionally colored
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The dry side of a mix path has character even before wet signal is blended.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Dry-path coloration declaration tells QC that dry is not transparent by design.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Colored dry paths are valid for analog-style tools, but must be explicit.
knowledgeCards.fix: Declare dry-leg coloration or change the dry path to transparent.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: This is a product-definition decision, not a knob tweak.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Set mix dry and compare to bypass.

knowledgeCards.title: Latency mode is not explained
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin feels late or changes latency depending on quality settings.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Latency metadata tells hosts and users whether the plugin is live-friendly or mix/mastering-focused.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: High quality may need latency. Live use needs responsiveness.
knowledgeCards.fix: Declare latency behavior and label quality modes clearly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Latency is acceptable when users know what mode they are choosing.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Test live playing, parallel blend, and offline render.

knowledgeCards.title: Color behavior is not declared
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin intentionally changes tone, but QC may treat it like an accidental mismatch.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Capability declarations tell QC what kind of processor the creator built.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Declaring color preserves artistic intent while keeping safety checks honest.
knowledgeCards.fix: Declare the plugin as having intentional nonlinear color.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: This is not a tone fix; it is confirming the plugin type.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Confirm the color stage is intentional across defaults and presets.

knowledgeCards.title: Self-oscillation needs a label
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin can generate sound or grow feedback on its own.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Capability metadata tells QC that self-oscillation is intentional and should be safety-tested differently.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Self-oscillation is expressive, but it must be safe and clear.
knowledgeCards.fix: Declare self-oscillation and require feedback/output safety caps.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: This can be a feature only when the product says so.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Test maximum resonance/feedback with no input and with short hits.

knowledgeCards.title: Bright fizz appears
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: High notes, cymbals, or distortion create brittle fizz or metallic junk.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Oversampling and tone filtering help distortion behave cleanly at high frequencies.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Higher quality can use more CPU. Lower quality may sound rougher and more digital.
knowledgeCards.fix: Enable higher-quality processing, lower drive, or filter harsh high fizz.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Aliased digital edge can be intentional for bitcrush or harsh digital effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to high notes, cymbals, distorted vocals, and synth leads.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the bright fizz? It may be character, but it can sound broken on high notes.

knowledgeCards.title: Starts too dirty
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin loads with grit, fuzz, or edge before the user asks for it.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Drive controls how hard the color stage is pushed.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More drive adds attitude and density. Less drive keeps the starting sound cleaner.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower drive until the default has character without forcing grit.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A dirty starting tone can be the identity of a color box, tape, tube, or crush effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to vocals, bass, cymbals, and full mixes for grit or fuzzy edges.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the dirty start? It gives character, but users may expect a cleaner default.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive adds mud
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound gets thick, cloudy, or boxy in the low mids.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Drive and tone shaping decide where saturation adds weight.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More low-mid color feels warm and vintage. Too much can hide clarity.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce drive, add damping, or trim low-mid buildup.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Low-mid thickness can be intentional for vintage, lo-fi, or bass-forward sounds.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to 150-500 Hz body on vocals, guitars, bass, and full mixes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the extra thickness? It may sound warm but can cloud a mix.

knowledgeCards.title: Control range allows unsafe values
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A knob can be turned into clipping, silence, runaway feedback, or broken audio.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Control ranges define what users are allowed to save and automate.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Wide ranges feel powerful. Unsafe ranges make the product fragile.
knowledgeCards.fix: Clamp the range or add a protected advanced mode.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Dangerous values belong behind explicit safety confirmation and output caps.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep every control to min and max while QC monitors safety.

knowledgeCards.title: Knob travel feels wrong
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Most useful settings are cramped into one tiny part of the knob.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Taper maps knob movement to parameter movement.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: The right taper makes a knob feel musical. The wrong taper feels jumpy or dead.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use a log, exponential, or custom taper that matches how the ear hears the parameter.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Unusual tapers are okay for special macros if they feel intentional.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Move the knob slowly and mark where the useful sounds live.

knowledgeCards.title: Control units are wrong
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The value readout does not match what the control appears to do.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Units tell users whether they are changing dB, ms, Hz, percent, or a mode.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Clear units build trust. Wrong units teach the wrong mental model.
knowledgeCards.fix: Correct the displayed unit and tooltip for the saved parameter.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Abstract controls can avoid units, but wrong units should not ship.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Check each exposed control against its visible label and audible effect.

knowledgeCards.title: Impulse response starts late
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The cabinet, room, or effect feels delayed before it reacts.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The start of an IR should usually begin at the first meaningful sound.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: A delayed IR can create pre-delay. Hidden delay makes the plugin feel late.
knowledgeCards.fix: Trim silence from the start or declare it as pre-delay.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A late-starting space can be intentional for rhythmic ambience.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sharp transients and compare bypass timing.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the late start? It should be labeled as pre-delay or timing character.

knowledgeCards.title: Impulse response changes level too much
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Loading an IR makes the plugin suddenly louder or quieter.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: IR gain normalizes captured responses so presets feel consistent.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Natural captures vary in level. Normalized captures are easier to browse.
knowledgeCards.fix: Normalize IR gain or save preset-specific wet/output trims.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Some IRs may intentionally be louder if they represent a special effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Switch through IR presets while a loop plays.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the IR level jump? Users may be surprised while browsing.

knowledgeCards.title: Impulse response plays at the wrong pitch
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The room, cab, or resonance sounds shifted, strange, or wrong at different sessions.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: IR sample-rate handling keeps captured audio playing at the intended speed.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: There is no musical upside to accidental IR rate mismatch.
knowledgeCards.fix: Resample or tag IRs correctly before they enter the plugin definition.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Pitch-shifted IRs should be a named effect, not a hidden import bug.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare the same IR at 44.1, 48, and 96 kHz.

knowledgeCards.title: Impulse response is too long
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The space or cabinet keeps ringing longer than the plugin promises.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: IR length decides how much captured response is played back.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Longer IRs sound more real. Shorter IRs are tighter and cheaper to run.
knowledgeCards.fix: Trim the IR tail or lower the decay/tail amount.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Long IR tails are valid for realistic spaces and cinematic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use short clicks, snare hits, and stopped guitar chords.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the long capture? It will make the plugin feel more spacious and less tight.

knowledgeCards.title: Ducked delay disappears
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Repeats vanish while the source plays and return too late.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Ducking lowers delay while the dry signal is present.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More ducking keeps vocals clear. Less ducking makes echoes more obvious.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce duck amount or speed up release.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Strong ducking is useful for clean pop vocals and throws.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocal phrases with spaces between lines.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy duck? Delay will hide behind the source.

knowledgeCards.title: Feedback tone gets sharper each repeat
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Each repeat becomes brighter, harsher, or more resonant.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Feedback filtering shapes every repeat before it loops back.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Regenerating tone creates dub character. Uncontrolled buildup gets piercing.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce resonance, add damping, or cap feedback tone.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Repeat tone buildup can be a dub performance feature.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play one hit and count how the tone changes across repeats.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the buildup? It needs to stay safe.

knowledgeCards.title: Repeats run away
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Delay repeats keep getting louder or never settle down.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Feedback sends repeats back into the delay.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: High feedback creates long echoes. Safety limits prevent dangerous buildup.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower feedback or add a feedback safety cap.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Self-oscillation must be a labeled performance mode with a cap.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play a short hit and listen for whether repeats fade or grow.

knowledgeCards.title: Ping-pong leans to one side
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Repeats are louder or more frequent on one side.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Ping-pong routing balances repeats between left and right.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Balanced ping-pong feels clean. Uneven ping-pong can feel human or weird.
knowledgeCards.fix: Balance left/right repeat levels and timing.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Uneven stereo echo can be a creative movement choice.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use a short mono hit and follow the repeats left/right.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the lean? It will move the stereo image.

knowledgeCards.title: Repeats are too sharp
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Each repeat stays bright, pokey, or distracting.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: High cut and damping shape how repeats fade into the background.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Brighter repeats stay clear. Darker repeats sit behind the source.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower repeat brightness or add damping in the feedback path.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Bright repeats can be intentional for rhythmic echoes, slapback, or dub-style throws.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to vocal throws, guitar plucks, and snare delays.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep bright repeats? They may cut through, but can distract from the dry sound.

knowledgeCards.title: Repeats disappear too fast
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Delay repeats lose clarity and vanish into darkness.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Damping decides how quickly repeats lose high frequency detail.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Darker repeats are smooth and vintage. Brighter repeats are clearer and more rhythmic.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce damping or open the high cut until repeats remain audible.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Very dark repeats can be perfect for tape-style or background echo.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to whether the second and third repeats are still musically useful.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep dark repeats? They may sit nicely, but users may expect more audible echo.

knowledgeCards.title: Old delay tail carries over
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The previous preset keeps echoing after switching sounds.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Preset transition behavior decides whether tails keep ringing or reset.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Keeping tails sounds smooth. Clearing tails avoids confusion when changing presets.
knowledgeCards.fix: Clear or fade tails when switching presets unless tail carryover is intentional.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Tail carryover can be musical for live performance or ambient effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Switch presets after a loud echo or reverb tail.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep tails across preset changes? It can be smooth, but users may hear the old preset bleeding into the new one.

knowledgeCards.title: Delay timing feels off
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Repeats do not land where the groove expects.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Tempo sync maps delay time to the song tempo and note division.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Strict sync locks to the beat. Free timing feels human or experimental.
knowledgeCards.fix: Set division/sync mode to match the intended groove.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Loose timing can be intentional for tape echo, slap, and experimental movement.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to repeats against drums or a metronome.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the loose timing? It may not line up with tempo.

knowledgeCards.title: Delay time clicks when moved
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Changing delay time creates clicks, zips, or hard jumps.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Time smoothing decides how delay changes happen while audio is running.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Smooth changes create pitch glide. Instant changes create glitch.
knowledgeCards.fix: Smooth delay-time changes or label glitch behavior clearly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Glitchy time jumps can be an experimental delay feature.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Move delay time while repeats are audible.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the glitch? It should be presented as a deliberate mode.

knowledgeCards.title: Echo wobbles too much
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Repeats wobble in pitch or feel seasick.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Wow/flutter adds tape-like pitch movement.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More wobble feels vintage and unstable. Less wobble keeps timing clearer.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce modulation depth or slow movement.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Heavy wobble is useful for lo-fi, tape, and psychedelic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sustained notes and repeating chords.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the wobble? It is a strong flavor.

knowledgeCards.title: Digital fizz on bright notes
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: High notes, cymbals, or vocal consonants create brittle metallic fizz.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Oversampling and filtering keep nonlinear distortion smoother at high frequencies.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Higher quality sounds smoother but uses more CPU. Lower quality sounds rougher.
knowledgeCards.fix: Enable oversampling, reduce drive, or filter harsh artifacts.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Digital fizz can be intentional for bitcrush, cyber, or harsh FX presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Try bright synth notes, cymbals, and vocal S sounds.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the fizz? This should be framed as an obvious digital character choice.
mustFixIf: plugin is marketed as clean saturation

knowledgeCards.title: Bias makes the tone lopsided
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The distortion feels spitty, uneven, or gated.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Bias changes which side of the waveform distorts more.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More bias creates spit and character. Less bias sounds smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Move bias closer to center or make the default less extreme.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Spitty bias is useful for fuzz, broken gear, and vintage character.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Hold single notes and listen for sputter or uneven decay.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the lopsided bias? It is character, not cleanliness.

knowledgeCards.title: Clipper ceiling crushes everything
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Peaks flatten constantly and the sound loses punch.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Clip ceiling sets where the waveform is flattened.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Lower ceiling catches more peaks. Higher ceiling keeps more transient life.
knowledgeCards.fix: Raise the clip ceiling or reduce input into the clipper.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Heavy clipping can be intentional for loud drums or aggressive masters.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kicks, snares, bass, and full mix peaks.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy clipping? It will reduce punch on purpose.

knowledgeCards.title: Asymmetry pulls the waveform off-center
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The tone may click, lose headroom, or feel uneven after heavy drive.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Bias changes the distortion shape; DC filtering keeps the waveform centered.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Asymmetry adds tube-like character. Too much offset wastes headroom.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add or tune DC blocking after asymmetric stages.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Asymmetry is musical, but unmanaged DC offset should not ship.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sustained bass and watch for headroom loss or clicks.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the asymmetric tone only if DC is safely controlled.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive knob does nothing at first
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The first part of the knob barely changes the sound.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Knob taper decides how useful each part of the range feels.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fine control is good. Dead zones make the plugin feel broken.
knowledgeCards.fix: Remap the drive curve so the useful range starts earlier.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A subtle lower range is okay only if the plugin is clearly a subtle saturator.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Move drive slowly from 0 to 100.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive jumps too quickly
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A tiny knob move causes a huge tone change.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Control taper determines how easy it is to dial in a sound.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Sensitive controls feel powerful. Smooth controls are easier for musicians.
knowledgeCards.fix: Soften the taper or add finer control around common settings.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme sensitivity can be acceptable for experimental tools if labeled.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Try tiny knob moves around 10, 25, 50, and 75 percent.

knowledgeCards.title: Fuzz cuts off note tails
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Notes choke or stop suddenly instead of fading.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Gate and bias determine how long fuzz keeps reacting as the note decays.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Gated fuzz is punchy and broken. Open sustain feels smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower gate amount, adjust bias, or increase sustain.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Gated decay is classic fuzz character.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Hold notes until they fade into silence.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the choking tail? It is a deliberate fuzz behavior.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive adds scratchy top end
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The top end becomes scratchy, sandy, or tiring.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: High-frequency shaping decides how much edge survives after distortion.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More edge cuts through. Less edge is smoother and easier to mix.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower presence, add high cut, or soften the drive curve.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Scratchy top can be intentional for punk, industrial, and noise effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use cymbals, vocals, distorted synths, and guitars.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the scratch? It will be an obvious aggressive texture.

knowledgeCards.title: Input hits the distortion too hard
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin sounds crushed immediately, even before the user turns the drive up.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Input level decides how hard the source hits the distortion stage.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More input creates hair and density. Less input keeps touch, punch, and clean-to-dirty range.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower input or drive until the default has usable range.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A hot input can define a fuzz, crush, or aggressive color plugin.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play soft and loud notes and check whether the tone reacts to performance.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hot drive? This makes the plugin start dirty by design.

knowledgeCards.title: Chords turn into smear
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Multiple notes blur into a wall instead of staying separated.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Heavy nonlinear drive creates new tones between notes.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More smear feels thick. More definition keeps harmony readable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce drive, trim low mids, or add definition.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Smear is useful for shoegaze, doom, noise, and pads.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Test chords, not only single notes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the smear? It makes harmony less clear.

knowledgeCards.title: Bass gets flubby
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Low notes lose shape and become woolly or loose.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Pre-drive bass decides how much low end gets distorted.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More bass into drive feels huge. Less bass keeps notes tight.
knowledgeCards.fix: Trim low end before the drive stage or add a Tight control.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Flubby low end can be intentional for doom, fuzz, lo-fi, or blown-speaker sounds.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use bass notes, kick drums, and low guitar palm mutes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the flub? It is a vibe, but it may blur the low end.

knowledgeCards.title: Bass disappears when driven
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The distorted tone loses weight and sounds small.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Low cut, blend, and tone controls decide how much body survives distortion.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Less bass avoids mud. More bass keeps power.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower the low cut or restore clean low end with a blend path.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Thin distortion can be correct for telephone, radio, or special-effect presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare low notes with the plugin on and off.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the thin drive? It removes weight on purpose.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive adds mud
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound gets thick but cloudy around the body of the tone.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Post-drive tone shaping controls how much low-mid buildup remains.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Low mids add warmth and size. Too much hides clarity.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce low-mid buildup after the drive stage.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Mud can be intentional for dark vintage color or lo-fi samples.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, guitars, bass, and full drum loops.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the extra thickness? It may feel warm but can crowd the mix.

knowledgeCards.title: Noise rises with drive
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Hiss or hum becomes obvious as drive increases.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Drive can amplify modeled noise along with music.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Noise adds vintage realism. Too much noise feels broken.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower default noise, add noise trim, or gate noise during silence.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Noise can be intentional for tape, vinyl, amp, or lo-fi plugins.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Stop playback or play quiet passages and listen for hiss.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the noise? It should feel like intentional texture.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive also gets louder
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Turning drive up sounds better partly because it also raises volume.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Output compensation keeps tone changes fair to compare.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Uncompensated drive feels exciting. Matched drive helps users judge tone honestly.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add or tune output compensation so drive does not create a huge loudness jump.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Some pedals and old hardware really do get louder when pushed.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep drive while watching whether level jumps more than tone changes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the drive boost? Users may hear volume as quality.

knowledgeCards.title: Drive gets smaller as it gets dirtier
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The dirty sound falls backward when drive increases.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Auto-level can turn the output down as distortion adds loudness.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Strong compensation protects headroom. Too much makes the effect feel weak.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce compensation strength until drive feels level-matched, not smaller.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A quieter dirty tone can work for background texture presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep drive on drums, bass, and vocals and listen for loss of presence.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the quieter dirty setting? It will feel less forward as it gets dirtier.

knowledgeCards.title: Parallel distortion gets hollow
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The tone gets thinner around the middle of the blend knob.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Parallel blend combines clean and distorted paths.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Parallel drive keeps clarity. Misaligned paths can cancel body.
knowledgeCards.fix: Align latency/phase or use a phase-safe blend path.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hollow phase tone can be a special effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep mix from dry to wet and focus around 50%.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hollow blend? It may sound weak in mono.

knowledgeCards.title: Octave artifact takes over
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: An octave-like overtone becomes louder than the source.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Rectification flips part of the waveform and creates strong octave harmonics.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More rectification is wild and synthetic. Less keeps the original note clearer.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower rectification or blend more dry tone.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Octave fuzz is a classic sound if the user expects it.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use single-note lines and chords separately.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the octave bite? Chords may get messy.

knowledgeCards.title: Soft clipper lacks edge
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound warms up but never gets the expected bite.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Softness changes how gradually the waveform bends into clipping.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Softer clipping is smooth. Harder clipping cuts more.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use a firmer curve or increase presence/drive range.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Soft clipping is ideal for gentle glue and mastering tone.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use drum bus, bass, and synth leads.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the soft behavior? It is warmth more than obvious distortion.

knowledgeCards.title: One side distorts more
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The stereo image leans or one speaker sounds dirtier.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Stereo drive routing decides whether both sides saturate equally.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Linked drive is stable. Unlinked drive can feel wide and animated.
knowledgeCards.fix: Link drive amount or balance left/right saturation.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Uneven drive can imitate analog imperfection.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use centered vocals, bass, and drums.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the uneven drive? The center image may move.

knowledgeCards.title: Wavefolder gets chaotic
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound jumps into complex metallic overtones very quickly.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Wavefolding bends the waveform back on itself to create rich harmonics.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More fold creates wild harmonics. Less fold keeps the source recognizable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce fold amount or smooth the fold curve near the default.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Chaotic folding can be the whole point of an experimental synth effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Try sine waves, bass, vocals, and simple synth leads.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the chaos? It should be marketed as an effect, not subtle color.

knowledgeCards.title: Attack flattens punch
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Drums, bass plucks, or consonants lose their first snap.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Attack decides how quickly compression grabs the sound.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast attack gives control and smash. Slower attack keeps punch.
knowledgeCards.fix: Slow the attack until the front edge returns.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Fast attack is useful for crushed drums and dense vocals.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to kick beater, snare crack, bass pluck, and vocal consonants.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the flattened punch? It is a controlled, smashed sound.

knowledgeCards.title: Peaks slip through
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Sharp hits poke through before control starts.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Attack decides how quickly gain reduction begins.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Slower attack keeps excitement. Faster attack catches peaks.
knowledgeCards.fix: Speed up attack or add peak catching.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Letting peaks through can preserve natural impact.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use snare hits, plosives, pick attack, and bass spikes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the loose attack? It may leave peaks less controlled.

knowledgeCards.title: Auto release feels wrong
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The compressor recovers naturally on some material but awkwardly on others.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Auto release changes recovery based on program level and timing.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Program-dependent release can sound musical. Wrong curves feel unpredictable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Retune the release curve or choose a better time constant default.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Strange recovery can be a deliberate vintage behavior.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Test sparse vocals, drum loops, bass, and full mix.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the unusual recovery? It should feel like character, not confusion.

knowledgeCards.title: De-esser makes vocals lispy
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: S sounds get dull, smeared, or unnatural.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: De-essing reduces harsh sibilant frequencies.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More de-essing controls harshness. Too much damages articulation.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce amount or retune the sibilance frequency.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A lispy vocal is usually not desired unless it is a special effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use words with S, SH, CH, T, and F sounds.

knowledgeCards.title: Gate chatters
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound opens and closes rapidly near silence.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Gate timing controls how smoothly quiet signal is muted.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Tight gates remove noise. Smoother gates preserve natural tails.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add hold/hysteresis or slow release.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Chatter is rarely useful outside a deliberate glitch mode.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Let drums, guitars, vocals, and reverb tails decay.

knowledgeCards.title: Gate cuts off tails
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Reverb, cymbals, vocals, or guitar sustain stops too abruptly.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Gate threshold and release decide when quiet tails are muted.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Tight gating cleans noise. Looser gating keeps natural decay.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower threshold, add hold, or slow release.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hard gating is valid for drums, stutter, and rhythmic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to the end of notes and room tails.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hard cut? It should feel rhythmic or intentional.

knowledgeCards.title: Compression grabs suddenly
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Compression appears suddenly instead of easing in.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Knee controls how gradually compression starts around the threshold.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Hard knee is assertive. Soft knee is smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Soften the knee or raise threshold slightly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A hard grab can be great for drums and aggressive control.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen around the moment gain reduction begins.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the grab? It makes compression more obvious.

knowledgeCards.title: Limiter misses sharp peaks
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Loud hits can clip or distort after export.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: True-peak limiting catches peaks between samples.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More safety can sound slightly less open. Missed peaks break release safety.
knowledgeCards.fix: Enable true-peak/oversampled limiting or lower ceiling.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Unsafe peaks should not be a creative keep.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Test loud drums, bright masters, and offline export.

knowledgeCards.title: Meter does not match the sound
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The meter suggests one thing while the audio sounds like another.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Meter mapping decides what the user sees when compression happens.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Meters do not need to be perfectly literal, but they must help users trust the plugin.
knowledgeCards.fix: Align the meter with the gain reduction or behavior it claims to show.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A stylized meter can be acceptable only if it is clearly labeled as vibe, not measurement.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Watch the meter while loud hits trigger compression.
mustFixIf: meter claims gain reduction but does not respond
meter moves when audio is bypassed

knowledgeCards.title: Meter does not match the sound
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The meter says one thing while the audio does another.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Meter mapping teaches users what the processor is doing.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Stylized meters are fine, but misleading meters break trust.
knowledgeCards.fix: Align the meter with the behavior it claims to show.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A vibe meter must be clearly labeled as vibe.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Watch the meter while loud hits trigger processing.

knowledgeCards.title: Ratio squeezes too hard
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The performance feels pinned, smaller, or less expressive.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Ratio decides how strongly loud parts are turned down.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Higher ratio controls more. Lower ratio keeps more natural movement.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower ratio or reduce compression amount.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: High ratio is right for limiting, smash, and effect compression.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, drum bus, bass, and full mix.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy ratio? It is control over natural movement.

knowledgeCards.title: Release pumps
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound breathes, bounces, or swells after loud hits.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Release decides how quickly compression lets go.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast release adds energy. Slower release sounds smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Slow or smooth release until movement feels musical.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Pumping is a signature dance, drum, and parallel compression sound.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen after kicks, snares, bass notes, and vocal phrases.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the pump? It is musical movement, but it will be obvious.

knowledgeCards.title: Release holds too long
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound stays tucked down after the loud moment is gone.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Release controls how long gain reduction remains.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Slow release is smooth and glued. Too slow feels pinned down.
knowledgeCards.fix: Shorten release until the sound recovers naturally.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Long release can be ideal for leveling vocals or bass.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen for whether the body returns before the next hit.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy hold? It makes the sound smoother but less lively.

knowledgeCards.title: Bass pushes the compressor too hard
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Kick or bass makes the whole mix duck too much.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The detector decides what part of the sound controls compression.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Low-end sensitivity creates pump. Filtered detection keeps the mix steady.
knowledgeCards.fix: Raise sidechain high-pass or reduce low-frequency detector sensitivity.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Bass-triggered pumping is intentional in dance and sidechain effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to the mix when kick and bass hit.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the bass pump? It will move the whole mix.

knowledgeCards.title: Low end pushes too hard
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Bass or kick makes the whole compressor duck too much.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The sidechain or detector decides what part of the sound tells the compressor to move.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More low-end sensitivity gives strong pumping. Less low-end sensitivity keeps the mix steadier.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce low-end sensitivity or raise the sidechain high-pass/DC threshold.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Bass-triggered pumping can be intentional for dance music and sidechain-style movement.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to how the rest of the mix moves when the kick or bass hits.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the low-end pump? It can add groove, but it may pull the whole mix around.

knowledgeCards.title: Compression barely reacts
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The compressor does not seem to change the sound much.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Threshold decides when the compressor starts reducing level.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Higher threshold keeps the source natural. Lower threshold makes compression more audible.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower the threshold or increase input until the compressor reacts when expected.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Subtle compression can be intentional for transparent mastering, bus glue, or gentle leveling.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen for gain reduction on loud phrases or hits.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the subtle behavior? It may be correct, but users may expect a stronger effect.

knowledgeCards.title: Too much compression
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound feels squeezed, smaller, or less alive.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Threshold decides how easily the compressor starts working.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Lower threshold gives more control and density. Higher threshold keeps more natural dynamics.
knowledgeCards.fix: Raise the threshold or reduce input drive until compression feels less constant.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Heavy compression may be intentional for crushed vocals, drums, or effect presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen for whether the performance still moves naturally.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy squeeze? It can sound vibey, but may reduce dynamics.

knowledgeCards.title: Technical detail belongs behind a drawer
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The user may be overwhelmed by words that do not connect to sound.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Advanced detail gives power users transparency without blocking musicians.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Simple language builds momentum. Technical detail supports trust.
knowledgeCards.fix: Show music-first guidance first and hide DSP details behind Advanced.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: The product should teach without forcing every user to become an engineer.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Check whether the user knows what to do without opening Advanced.

knowledgeCards.title: Keep decision needs A/B proof
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The creator may keep a choice without comparing the safer alternative.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: A/B comparison teaches the audible cost of a creative keep.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: The user stays in control, but the product makes the tradeoff visible.
knowledgeCards.fix: Show before, suggested fix, and keep-as-is comparison before saving intent.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A creative keep is stronger when the user has heard the alternative.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use the exact source that caused the finding plus one musical example.

knowledgeCards.title: Envelope follower jumps around
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Filter, volume, or effect amount jitters instead of following the groove.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Envelope followers turn input loudness into movement.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast followers feel reactive. Smoother followers feel musical.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add smoothing, slow attack/release, or reduce sensitivity.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Jumpy following can be valid for glitch and rhythmic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use drums, bass, vocals, and uneven performance dynamics.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the jumpy movement? It will feel reactive and possibly nervous.

knowledgeCards.title: Envelope follower reacts too late
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The movement misses the front of notes and arrives after the groove.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Follower attack controls how quickly movement reacts to loudness.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Slower movement is smooth. Faster movement tracks transients.
knowledgeCards.fix: Speed up attack or reduce smoothing.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Slow following can be intentional for swelling effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use plucked bass, guitar, drums, and vocal phrases.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the lazy follow? It will feel smoother but less precise.

knowledgeCards.title: Dynamic EQ pumps the tone
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A frequency band ducks or blooms in an obvious, distracting way.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Dynamic EQ changes a frequency only when it crosses a level.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More movement controls problem tones. Less movement sounds natural.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce range or smooth timing.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Obvious tonal pumping can be a creative movement effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, bass, cymbals, and full mix.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the moving tone? It will feel like an effect.

knowledgeCards.title: High cut dulls the sound
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Air, brightness, pick detail, or vocal presence disappears.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: High cut removes brightness above a chosen point.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Lower high cut sounds warm. Too low sounds muffled.
knowledgeCards.fix: Open the high cut until enough detail returns.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Dark tone is valid for tape, vintage, underwater, or background sounds.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to cymbals, consonants, acoustic attack, and synth brightness.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the dark tone? It may hide detail.

knowledgeCards.title: Low cut thins the sound
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Bass, body, or warmth disappears.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Low cut removes low frequencies below a chosen point.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Higher low cut cleans rumble. Too high makes the source thin.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower the low cut until body returns while rumble stays controlled.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Thin tone is valid for telephone, lo-fi, radio, or special effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to bass, kick, vocal body, and guitar warmth.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the thin tone? It removes weight.

knowledgeCards.title: Tone rings or whistles
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A narrow frequency jumps out, whistles, or rings.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Resonance/Q controls how narrow and intense a tone boost is.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More resonance gives character. Too much can be harsh or unstable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower resonance/Q or reduce the boosted frequency.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Sharp resonance is valid for synth filters and sound design.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep notes, vocals, cymbals, and full mix through the filter.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the ringing tone? It may be harsh at some notes.

knowledgeCards.title: Exciter adds too much air
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The top end feels shiny, sharp, or fake.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Exciters add upper harmonics to create brightness.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More air adds detail. Too much becomes harsh or plastic.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower air/excite amount or target a narrower band.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extra air can be intentional for pop vocals and sparkle presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, cymbals, acoustic guitar, and full mix.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the shine? It may fatigue listeners.

knowledgeCards.title: Module feedback loop is unsafe
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A routed loop grows, screams, clips, or never settles.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Feedback routing sends processed audio back into earlier modules.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Feedback creates wild effects. Unsafe feedback can damage ears and sessions.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add feedback caps, damping, and output safety limits.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Self-oscillation must be a clearly labeled mode with a ceiling.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Stress test maximum feedback, drive, resonance, and wet mix.

knowledgeCards.title: Filter movement zippers
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Moving cutoff creates stair-step noise or zipper clicks.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Parameter smoothing keeps knob and automation movement fluid.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast response follows automation. Smoothing avoids rough stepping.
knowledgeCards.fix: Smooth cutoff changes without making automation feel sluggish.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Zipper noise should not be part of normal filter movement.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep cutoff slowly and automate it quickly.

knowledgeCards.title: Filter starts whistling
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The filter creates its own loud tone or whistle.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Resonance boosts frequencies around the cutoff and can self-oscillate.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: High resonance is expressive. Uncontrolled resonance can be piercing.
knowledgeCards.fix: Limit resonance level or clearly mark self-oscillation as a mode.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Self-oscillation is valid for synth-style filters if controlled.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep cutoff at high resonance and watch peak level.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep self-oscillation? It needs a safe cap.

knowledgeCards.title: Freeze clicks when captured
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Engaging freeze creates a click, pop, or hard edge.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Freeze capture needs crossfade/smoothing to avoid sudden waveform cuts.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast capture feels responsive. Smooth capture avoids artifacts.
knowledgeCards.fix: Crossfade into the frozen buffer or smooth capture boundaries.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Freeze should not pop unless it is explicitly a glitch mode.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Trigger freeze during sustained vocals, pads, and reverb tails.

knowledgeCards.title: Grains click
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The texture has little clicks or ticks between grains.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Grain windowing and overlap smooth the edges of each slice.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Sharper grains sound crisp. Smoother grains sound blended.
knowledgeCards.fix: Increase window smoothing or overlap.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Clicks can be intentional for glitch textures if labeled.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sustained pads, vocals, and long samples.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the clicks? It should be a glitch style.

knowledgeCards.title: Tempo changes break timing
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Delay, tremolo, or sequenced effects drift, jump, or click when tempo changes.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Host sync keeps time-based effects locked to the DAW tempo map.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Tempo-following should remain musical across changes.
knowledgeCards.fix: Smooth tempo changes and recalculate synced timing safely.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Tempo chaos should be a named special effect, not normal sync behavior.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Automate tempo while a synced effect is running.

knowledgeCards.title: Playback start clicks
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Starting playback creates a click or abrupt state jump.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Transport reset controls how internal envelopes, LFOs, and buffers start.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Reset makes timing predictable. Smoothing prevents audible jumps.
knowledgeCards.fix: Fade or smooth internal state on transport start.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Clicks on playback start are rarely musical.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Start and stop playback repeatedly with audio held through the plugin.

knowledgeCards.title: Output clips on loud sounds
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Loud hits crackle, flatten, or distort in an uncontrolled way.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Output, ceiling, and safety limiting keep the plugin from creating broken digital peaks.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Lowering output may feel less loud, but it protects headroom and prevents harsh clipping.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower output or add safe limiting until peaks stop clipping.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Creative distortion belongs in a controlled drive stage, not accidental output clipping.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to loud kicks, snares, bass notes, and full-mix peaks.

knowledgeCards.title: Makeup gain overshoots
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Compression makes the signal jump forward instead of simply controlling it.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Makeup gain adds level after compression to replace level that was reduced.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More makeup feels powerful, but too much hides whether the compression is actually helping.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce makeup gain or turn down auto makeup strength.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extra makeup may be desired for aggressive parallel or smash presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare bypassed and active level after gain reduction starts.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the extra push? This makes the compressor feel louder than the source.

knowledgeCards.title: Output jumps up
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin sounds better partly because it gets louder.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Output trim sets the level people hear when the plugin loads or when a preset is selected.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More output can feel exciting, but it can fool the ear and make fair comparison harder.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower the output until the processed signal feels level-matched with the input.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A louder default can be intentional for an effect plugin, but the creator should know it is part of the sound.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Toggle the plugin on and off and listen for loudness jump, not tone.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep this louder starting point? Users may think the plugin sounds better because it is louder.

knowledgeCards.title: One preset is much louder
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: One preset jumps out much louder than the rest.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Preset output sets the saved loudness for only that preset.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: A hotter preset can feel dramatic, but it can surprise users when switching sounds.
knowledgeCards.fix: Bring the preset output closer to the pack average.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A preset named Crush, Smash, or Lead Boost may intentionally be louder.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Switch between presets and listen for sudden jumps.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep this preset hotter than the rest? It may surprise users during preset browsing.

knowledgeCards.title: LFO reset jumps
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Movement jumps, clicks, or starts awkwardly when playback begins.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Phase reset decides where the movement cycle starts.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Reset gives repeatable timing. Free phase is smoother and less predictable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Smooth phase reset or make retrigger optional.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hard retrigger can be useful for rhythmic EDM effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Start/stop playback with tempo-synced movement enabled.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the retrigger? It will be a rhythmic behavior.

knowledgeCards.title: Modulation drifts from tempo
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Movement starts in time but slowly falls away from the beat.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Tempo sync locks modulation rate to host tempo.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Locked sync feels rhythmic. Free-running LFOs feel organic.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use host-synced timing or label the mode as free-run.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Free drift can be musical for chorus, phaser, and ambient motion.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use tremolo, auto-pan, or filter movement against a click.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the drift? It will not stay grid-locked.

knowledgeCards.title: Linear-phase mode rings before hits
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A soft ghost of the sound appears before drums or sharp notes.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Linear-phase processing can preserve phase but create pre-ringing around transients.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Linear phase preserves alignment. Minimum phase often feels punchier.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use minimum-phase mode for transient-heavy presets or reduce steep filtering.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Linear phase can be right for mastering and transparent tone shaping.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kick, snare, percussion, and sharp piano notes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep linear phase? It may trade punch for phase accuracy.

knowledgeCards.title: Old audio remains in the loop buffer
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Previous audio appears in a new preset, new capture, or fresh playback.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Buffer reset clears stored audio when the user expects a fresh state.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Keeping buffers can be musical only when spillover is explicit.
knowledgeCards.fix: Clear buffers on preset/load/reset or expose spillover as a labeled choice.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hidden old audio should not appear in a new sound.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Capture audio, switch presets, clear, and start playback again.

knowledgeCards.title: Macro controls fight each other
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Turning one macro undoes, exaggerates, or scrambles another control.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Macros can move many hidden parameters at once.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Macros make complex plugins simple. Conflicting macros make them confusing.
knowledgeCards.fix: Separate macro targets or define a clear priority when controls overlap.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Interactive macros are okay when the UI explains the relationship.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Move each macro alone, then together, while listening for predictable changes.

knowledgeCards.title: Morph knob sounds bad in the middle
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The endpoints sound good, but halfway sounds hollow, too loud, or messy.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Morphing moves through saved states, not only the start and end.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Dramatic morphs can pass through wild places. Smooth morphs are easier to perform.
knowledgeCards.fix: Retune the midpoint or add a safer curve through the morph path.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A strange midpoint can be intentional for performance effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep the morph slowly from A to B on multiple sources.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the strange middle? It becomes a performance effect.

knowledgeCards.title: Macro range is too wide
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A small move makes the plugin jump from subtle to extreme.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Macro range sets how far hidden controls move.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Wide macros are exciting. Useful macros give musicians control.
knowledgeCards.fix: Narrow the macro range around musical values.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme macros need a clear name like Destroy or Chaos.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Move the macro in tiny steps across common settings.

knowledgeCards.title: Gain reduction meter moves the wrong way
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The display suggests less compression when more compression is happening.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Gain reduction meters show how much level is being turned down.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Meters can be stylized, but direction must be understandable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Correct meter direction and labeling.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: An inverted GR meter breaks user trust.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Drive the compressor harder and verify the meter shows more reduction.

knowledgeCards.title: Peak meter misses fast hits
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The meter looks safe while sharp hits may still clip.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Meter ballistics decide how quickly the display reacts.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Smooth meters are easy to read. Peak meters need fast detection.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use true peak/peak hold for safety readings and VU for average loudness.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A slow vibe meter is fine only if not labeled as peak.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sharp drums, clicks, and clipped test material.

knowledgeCards.title: VU meter calibration is wrong
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The meter suggests healthy level while the plugin is too hot or too quiet.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: VU calibration maps average level to the meter's reference point.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Different references are fine when labeled. Wrong calibration misleads users.
knowledgeCards.fix: Set and display the VU reference level clearly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Stylized meters should not pretend to be calibrated tools.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Feed known -18 dBFS and -12 dBFS test tones and pink noise.

knowledgeCards.title: MIDI trigger fires twice
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: An envelope, chop, or effect starts twice from one note.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Trigger logic decides when musical events start.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Retriggering can be expressive. Accidental double firing feels broken.
knowledgeCards.fix: Debounce note triggers or separate note-on from transport reset.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Double triggers need to be an explicit ratchet or roll mode.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play single MIDI notes and repeated notes at different speeds.

knowledgeCards.title: Velocity response is too extreme
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Soft and hard playing jump too far apart in tone or volume.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Velocity mapping turns performance strength into parameter movement.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More sensitivity feels expressive. Too much is hard to control.
knowledgeCards.fix: Soften the velocity curve or reduce modulation depth.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme velocity response can be valid for performance instruments.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play quiet, medium, and hard notes through the same preset.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the wide response? It will reward or punish touch strongly.

knowledgeCards.title: Stereo gets weak in mono
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound gets smaller, thinner, or disappears when summed to mono.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Width and stereo spread decide how left and right signals relate to each other.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More width feels bigger in stereo. Mono-safe width keeps the core sound intact everywhere.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce risky width or enable mono-safe mode.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme width can be intentional for headphones or special effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare stereo to mono and listen for loss of center, bass, or vocal body.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the wide sound? It may lose strength on mono speakers or clubs.

knowledgeCards.title: Mix sounds hollow at 50%
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound gets thin, hollow, or phasey around the middle of the mix knob.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The mix path decides how dry and wet signals combine.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Parallel tone can create character. Misaligned paths cancel important sound.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use a phase-safe or latency-aligned blend path.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hollow phase tone can be a special effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep the mix knob from dry to wet and listen around 50%.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hollow blend? It may sound weak in mono.
mustFixIf: dry at 0 percent is not dry

knowledgeCards.title: Mix at dry is not dry
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin still changes the sound when mix is fully dry.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The dry end of a mix knob should let the original signal through untouched.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: A dry label needs to mean dry.
knowledgeCards.fix: Fix the mix path so 0% mix is fully dry.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: If dry is intentionally colored, call it Color or Console, not Dry.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Set mix to 0% and compare to bypass.

knowledgeCards.title: Mix at wet still has dry signal
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The original sound remains even when the mix says fully wet.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The wet end of a mix knob should usually show only the processed signal.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Keeping some dry can make effects clearer, but users may expect true wet-only behavior.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce dry contribution at 100% wet.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Some compressors and color tools are never truly wet-only by design.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Set mix to 100% and listen for unchanged dry signal.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep dry signal in the wet setting? Make sure the control behavior matches user expectations.

knowledgeCards.title: Auto-pan loses the center
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The source moves so wide that the middle feels empty.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Pan depth moves signal between left and right speakers.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More depth creates motion. Less depth keeps a stable center.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce depth or preserve some center signal.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Full-width auto-pan is great for ear candy and movement effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, percussion, synth plucks, and mono compatibility.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the wide pan? The center may feel empty.

knowledgeCards.title: Chorus gets weak in mono
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The chorus sounds wide in stereo but loses body in mono.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Stereo modulation can create left/right phase differences.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More width feels lush. Mono-safe width keeps the core tone strong.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce width or enable mono-safe chorus behavior.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme chorus width can be a headphone/special-effect choice.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare stereo and mono on guitars, keys, and vocals.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the wide chorus? It may weaken on mono systems.

knowledgeCards.title: Flanger whistles
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A sharp resonant whistle appears during the sweep.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Flanger feedback intensifies moving comb-filter peaks.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More feedback sounds dramatic. Too much becomes piercing.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower feedback or limit resonant peaks.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Jet-like whistle is valid for obvious flanger effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Sweep through the full range on bright material.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the whistle? It should be an obvious effect.

knowledgeCards.title: Movement feels seasick
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Pitch, tone, or stereo position moves so much it feels wobbly or distracting.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Depth sets how far the modulation moves.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More depth creates obvious motion. Less depth gives subtle width or shimmer.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce depth or mix until movement supports the sound.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme movement is useful for psychedelic, lo-fi, or special-effect presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to sustained vocals, pads, guitars, and keys.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy movement? It can be a strong effect, but may distract from the part.

knowledgeCards.title: Phaser hollows the sound
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The moving notches remove too much body.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Depth, stages, and feedback control how strong the phase notches are.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Deep notches are psychedelic. Shallower notches keep the source intact.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce depth, feedback, or wet mix.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Deep phasing is classic on keys, guitars, and psychedelic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use pads, guitar, vocal, and drum bus.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hollow sweep? It is a strong color.

knowledgeCards.title: Movement is too fast
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The effect vibrates, pulses, or wobbles faster than the music wants.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Rate controls how quickly the movement cycles.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast rate is obvious and energetic. Slow rate feels wider and smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Slow the rate or sync it to a musical division.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Fast modulation can be intentional for rotary, vibrato, tremolo, or sci-fi effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen against the song tempo and sustained notes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep fast movement? It can be exciting, but may fight the groove.

knowledgeCards.title: Rotary speed change feels wrong
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The speed shift feels too instant or too sluggish.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Ramp controls how the rotary speaker speeds up or slows down.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast ramp is responsive. Slow ramp feels more like physical hardware.
knowledgeCards.fix: Tune ramp time so speed changes feel musical.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Unusual ramp behavior can be a creative modulation signature.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Switch slow/fast while holding organ, guitar, or pad chords.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the ramp? It defines the performance feel.

knowledgeCards.title: Tremolo cuts too much volume
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound disappears too much during each pulse.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Tremolo depth controls how far volume moves up and down.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Deep tremolo is rhythmic and obvious. Shallow tremolo is gentle movement.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce depth or soften the waveform.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Deep chop is intentional for rhythmic guitar, synth, and stutter effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play sustained chords in time with a beat.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the chop? It will act like rhythm, not subtle movement.

knowledgeCards.title: Module order changes the musical result
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The same modules sound unexpectedly different because of their order.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Signal flow decides which processor hears the output of another processor.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Drive before compression behaves differently from compression before drive.
knowledgeCards.fix: Choose an order that matches the intended musical behavior and save it.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Unusual order is a valid sandbox choice if the creator understands it.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: A/B module order on drums, bass, and vocals.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep this order? It is part of the plugin identity.

knowledgeCards.title: Mid channel gets hollow
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Lead vocal, kick, bass, or snare loses body in the center.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Mid processing controls the center content of the stereo image.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Sculpting mid can make space. Too much weakens the song core.
knowledgeCards.fix: Restore mid body or reduce excessive side emphasis.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A hollow center can be an effect, but not a normal mix tool default.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use centered vocals, kick, bass, and mono checks.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hollow center? The mix core may feel weak.

knowledgeCards.title: Sides get too bright
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The stereo edges sparkle or hiss more than the center.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Side processing changes only the left-right difference information.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Bright sides feel wide. Too much side brightness distracts and can get phasey.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower side air/exciter or balance side EQ with the mid.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Bright sides can be intentional for wide pop or ambient effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare stereo, mono, headphones, and small speakers.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep bright sides? It may sound wide but less solid.

knowledgeCards.title: One band pumps the tone
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Bass, mids, or highs move separately in a distracting way.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Each band can compress independently from the others.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Independent bands solve problems. Too much movement sounds unnatural.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce band range or smooth timing.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Band pumping can be a creative tonal movement effect.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use full mix, drum bus, bass, and bright vocals.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the moving band? It will be audible tone animation.

knowledgeCards.title: Crossover creates a tonal dip
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A frequency range gets weaker around the band split.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Crossovers split audio into bands that must recombine cleanly.
