knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Multiband control is powerful. Bad recombination changes the tone even when idle.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use a matched crossover design or adjust band alignment.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A crossover dip should not happen when the processor is idle.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Bypass band processing and compare split/recombined audio to dry.

knowledgeCards.title: Multiband transient splits apart
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A drum hit feels like lows, mids, and highs arrive separately.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Band timing must preserve the feeling of one coherent transient.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Band-specific dynamics are powerful, but timing mismatches break punch.
knowledgeCards.fix: Align band latency/timing or use gentler crossover settings.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Split transients usually feel broken, not creative.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kick, snare, percussion, and slap bass.

knowledgeCards.title: Quality mode uses too much CPU
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin may crackle, drop out, or feel heavy in larger sessions.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Quality modes trade CPU cost for smoother or cleaner sound.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More quality can sound better. Lower CPU keeps sessions playable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add an Eco/Live mode or lower the default quality cost.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: High CPU can be acceptable for offline mastering modes.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Run multiple instances and test live playing plus offline export.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the heavy mode? It should be labeled as high-quality or offline.

knowledgeCards.title: Quality mode changes latency
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Switching quality mode makes the plugin feel later or changes parallel blend.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Oversampling can add delay that must be reported and compensated.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Higher quality reduces artifacts. Unannounced latency breaks timing trust.
knowledgeCards.fix: Report latency per mode and warn users when modes affect live feel.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Latency is fine when labeled and compensated.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Switch quality modes while checking parallel dry/wet alignment.

knowledgeCards.title: Voice shape sounds unnatural
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The voice sounds chipmunk, monster, hollow, or fake.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Formant controls vocal body separately from pitch.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Natural formants keep identity. Extreme formants create character.
knowledgeCards.fix: Move formant toward a more natural vocal body.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme formants are valid for character voices and effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use spoken words and sung vowels.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the voice shape? It is a character choice.

knowledgeCards.title: Harmony overpowers the lead
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Generated harmonies pull attention away from the main line.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Harmony level controls how much generated pitch is mixed in.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Louder harmonies sound dramatic. Lower harmonies support the lead.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower harmony level or narrow harmony range.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Dominant harmonies are valid for special FX and stacked vocal presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use a lead vocal or monophonic synth phrase.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the loud harmony? It becomes part of the lead sound.

knowledgeCards.title: Pitch tracking glitches
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Pitch jumps, warbles, or chooses wrong notes.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Pitch tracking decides what note the plugin thinks it hears.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fast tracking reacts quickly. Smoother tracking avoids jumps.
knowledgeCards.fix: Tune tracking speed, key/scale, or quality mode.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Glitchy pitch can be intentional for robotic or experimental effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, bass, and monophonic synth lines.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the glitches? It should sound intentional, not broken.

knowledgeCards.title: Default preset is not a safe start
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin opens loud, extreme, unstable, or confusing.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The default preset teaches users what normal behavior feels like.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Bold defaults show personality. Safe defaults earn trust first.
knowledgeCards.fix: Make the default clear, level-safe, and representative of the plugin.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: An extreme default can work for a dedicated special-effect plugin.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Load a fresh instance and listen before touching anything.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the bold default? It should be part of the product identity.

knowledgeCards.title: Preset name promises the wrong thing
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A preset named Clean, Warm, Wide, or Safe behaves differently than expected.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Preset names and tags tell users when to choose a sound.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Creative names can be fun. Misleading names make browsing harder.
knowledgeCards.fix: Rename the preset or adjust the sound to match the promise.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Abstract names are okay when they do not imply a technical behavior.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Browse presets by name and verify each one delivers what it says.

knowledgeCards.title: Preset change clicks
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Switching presets creates a click or pop.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Preset smoothing prevents sudden audio state changes.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Instant switching is quick. Smooth switching prevents artifacts.
knowledgeCards.fix: Crossfade or smooth state changes on preset switch.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Preset switching should not pop.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Switch presets during sustained audio and silence.

knowledgeCards.title: Preset change jumps in volume
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Switching presets creates a sudden loudness jump.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Preset output and transition smoothing decide how safe browsing feels.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Bold presets can have different energy. Huge jumps interrupt workflow.
knowledgeCards.fix: Normalize preset output or smooth transitions.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A boost preset can be louder if named clearly.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Click through presets while audio loops.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the jump? Users may be surprised while browsing.

knowledgeCards.title: Preset does not recall correctly
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Reloading a preset does not bring back the same sound.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Preset recall restores every saved authored parameter and hidden state.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: There is no musical upside to unreliable recall.
knowledgeCards.fix: Save and restore all parameters, modes, seeds, and hidden states used by the sound.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Generative behavior still needs explicit seed or free-random labeling.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Save, reload, switch away, switch back, and compare.

knowledgeCards.title: Random movement does not recall the same way
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The same preset sounds different every time it loads.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Random seed behavior controls whether chaos is repeatable or fresh each time.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Fresh random feels alive. Repeatable random keeps presets reliable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Save a seed per preset or label the movement as free random.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Non-repeatable random is valid for generative and ambient tools.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Reload the same preset multiple times and compare movement.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep free random? The preset will not sound exactly the same twice.

knowledgeCards.title: Randomizer creates unsafe settings
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Random presets can create clipping, silence, runaway feedback, or unusable extremes.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Randomizers need safe ranges so creative exploration stays playable.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Wide randomization discovers surprises. Safe bounds prevent broken sounds.
knowledgeCards.fix: Limit random ranges and exclude unsafe combinations.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Chaos should still stay inside release-safe boundaries.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Generate many random presets and run safety QC on each.

knowledgeCards.title: Space starts too soon or too late
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The reverb either swallows the source immediately or feels detached from it.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Pre-delay controls the gap between the dry sound and the start of the reverb.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Short pre-delay blends the sound into the room. Longer pre-delay keeps the source clearer.
knowledgeCards.fix: Adjust pre-delay until the source stays clear but still feels connected to the space.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme pre-delay can be intentional for slap-like ambience or special effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to vocal clarity, snare impact, and the moment the room appears.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep this space timing? It can be a vibe, but may feel detached or blurry.

knowledgeCards.title: Room slap is too loud
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The first room bounce sounds like a slap or echo.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Early reflections create the first sense of room size and walls.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Louder reflections define space. Softer reflections feel smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower early reflections or adjust room size/pre-delay.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Strong early reflections can define small room or slap ambience.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use snare, vocal, and handclaps.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the room slap? It will be part of the groove.

knowledgeCards.title: Reverb gets boomy
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The reverb adds low cloud or rumble.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Low damping and low cut decide how much bass lives in the tail.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More low end feels huge. Less low end keeps mixes clear.
knowledgeCards.fix: Raise reverb low cut or reduce low-frequency decay.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Boomy space can be intentional for cinematic, dub, or sound design presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kicks, toms, bass, and low vocals.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the boom? It may muddy the mix.

knowledgeCards.title: Reverb rings metallic
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The tail has metallic notes or ringing frequencies.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Diffusion, damping, and modulation shape how smooth the tail feels.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More character can sound vintage. Smoother tails sound natural.
knowledgeCards.fix: Increase diffusion/modulation or damp ringing frequencies.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Metallic reverb is valid for plates, springs, and sound design.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Send snare, vocal, and short clicks into the reverb.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the ring? It should feel like plate/spring character.

knowledgeCards.title: Reverb movement feels seasick
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The tail wobbles, swims, or pulls pitch too obviously.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Reverb modulation moves the delay network to avoid static ringing.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More modulation is lush. Too much distracts from the source.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce modulation depth or slow the rate.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Heavy modulation is valid for ambient and dream-pop wash.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sustained vocals, pads, piano, and guitar swells.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the swim? It is a strong space character.

knowledgeCards.title: Space feels detached
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The reverb arrives late enough to feel separate from the source.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Pre-delay sets the gap before the room appears.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More pre-delay keeps the source clear. Too much disconnects the space.
knowledgeCards.fix: Shorten pre-delay until the room feels connected.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Long pre-delay can create rhythmic slap ambience.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, snare, and short stabs.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the detached space? It should be rhythmically intentional.

knowledgeCards.title: Shimmer takes over
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The pitched reverb becomes louder than the original space.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Shimmer level controls how much pitched signal feeds the reverb.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More shimmer feels magical. Less shimmer keeps the source clear.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower shimmer level or feedback.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Huge shimmer is intentional for ambient and cinematic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, guitar swells, and pads.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the shimmer cloud? It may dominate the sound.

knowledgeCards.title: Reverb keeps building
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The reverb grows, blooms, or piles up instead of fading.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Decay and damping control how energy leaves the reverb.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Big bloom can be musical. Uncontrolled buildup is unsafe or muddy.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce regeneration, lower decay, add damping, or cap the tail.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Infinite/freeze modes must be explicit performance modes with safety controls.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Feed a short hit into the reverb and listen for whether the tail fades.

knowledgeCards.title: Reverb tail hangs too long
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The space keeps ringing after the source should be clear.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Decay controls how long the reverb tail lasts.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Long decay feels huge and cinematic. Shorter decay keeps the mix cleaner.
knowledgeCards.fix: Shorten decay or add damping until the tail clears naturally.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Long tails are intentional for ambient, cinematic, and wash effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen after vocals, snares, and stabs stop.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the long tail? It can be beautiful, but may blur the next phrase.

knowledgeCards.title: Left and right are swapped
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Left-side material comes out on the right, or stereo placement feels reversed.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Channel routing decides where left, right, mid, and side signals go.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Creative channel tricks should be explicit effects, not hidden mistakes.
knowledgeCards.fix: Correct the channel map so left and right stay where users expect.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A swap can be a special effect only if the control clearly says Swap.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use left-only and right-only test signals.

knowledgeCards.title: Sound feels late
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The processed sound feels delayed or out of time with the dry signal.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Lookahead, oversampling, and some effects add processing delay.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More quality can sound cleaner. Less latency feels tighter live.
knowledgeCards.fix: Compensate latency or use a low-latency mode.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Latency can be acceptable for mastering/offline modes if labeled.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare dry and processed transients in parallel.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the latency? It may not feel good for live playing.

knowledgeCards.title: Polarity flips unexpectedly
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound cancels when mixed with the original or feels hollow.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Polarity flips the waveform upside down.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Polarity flip is useful as a deliberate utility. Hidden flips break blending.
knowledgeCards.fix: Correct polarity or label the flip clearly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Intentional polarity flip is valid for utility and creative phase effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Blend with dry signal and check mono.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the flip? It must be labeled as a feature.

knowledgeCards.title: Preset goes silent
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A preset or setting produces no sound when it should pass audio.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Routing and output controls decide whether signal reaches the end of the plugin.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Mute effects should be explicit and labeled. Accidental silence is a broken preset.
knowledgeCards.fix: Restore the signal path or output level.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Intentional silence belongs in a gate, stutter, or mute effect with clear labeling.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play steady audio and switch through presets.

knowledgeCards.title: Bypass pops
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Turning the plugin on/off creates a click or pop.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Bypass smoothing avoids sudden waveform or level changes.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Instant bypass is fast. Smooth bypass protects speakers and ears.
knowledgeCards.fix: Crossfade or smooth bypass transitions.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Bypass should not pop.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Toggle bypass during silence, sustained notes, and loud audio.

knowledgeCards.title: Quiet tails cause CPU spikes
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: CPU rises or crackles appear when the sound is nearly silent.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Denormal protection keeps tiny quiet numbers from slowing DSP math.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: There is no musical downside to stable denormal protection.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add denormal guards in feedback, filters, and tails.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: CPU instability should not ship.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Run silence after long reverb/delay tails and watch CPU.

knowledgeCards.title: Setting can create a headphone spike
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A setting can suddenly blast a loud tone, pop, or squeal.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Safety limiting prevents accidental dangerous output while preserving creative range.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: No creative feature should surprise users with unsafe level.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add hard safety caps, ramping, and feedback/resonance bounds.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Dangerous spikes cannot be kept for release.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Stress all maximum feedback, resonance, drive, and preset switching combinations.

knowledgeCards.title: Effect memory grows over time
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Long sessions may slow down, crackle, or eventually fail.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Buffers and tails need fixed limits so the plugin stays stable.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Long captures are musical. Unbounded memory is not safe.
knowledgeCards.fix: Cap buffer length and clear unused stored audio.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Infinite capture needs explicit limits and user feedback.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Run long feedback, freeze, and looper tests for several minutes.

knowledgeCards.title: Audio engine becomes unstable
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin may blast, stop, crackle, or break the audio stream.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Safety guards prevent math and feedback from creating impossible audio values.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: There is no musical benefit to unstable output values.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add or adjust safety limits so the engine stays stable.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme sound design still needs stable output.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Stress test high feedback, high resonance, high drive, and preset changes.

knowledgeCards.title: Export sounds different from playback
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Exported audio does not match what the user heard during playback.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Render consistency keeps live playback and bounced audio aligned.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Offline can use higher quality, but it should not surprise the user.
knowledgeCards.fix: Make offline mode deterministic or clearly expose different render quality.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Unexpected render differences should not ship.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Compare real-time playback to offline export.

knowledgeCards.title: Tone changes at different sample rates
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin sounds different at 44.1, 48, or 96 kHz.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Sample-rate-aware DSP keeps timing and filters consistent.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Analog models may vary slightly, but core tone should stay predictable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Normalize timing/filter math across sample rates.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Large sample-rate tone changes are confusing and should be fixed.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Render the same preset at multiple sample rates.

knowledgeCards.title: Preset goes silent
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: A preset produces no sound when it should pass audio.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Routing and output controls decide whether signal reaches the end of the plugin.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Mute effects should be explicit and labeled. Accidental silence is broken.
knowledgeCards.fix: Restore the signal path or output level.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Intentional silence belongs in a gate, stutter, or mute effect with clear labeling.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Play steady audio and switch through presets.

knowledgeCards.title: Plugin creates subsonic rumble
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The mix may lose headroom or move speakers even when bass is not obvious.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Subsonic filtering removes energy below the useful musical range.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Deep low end is powerful. Unheard rumble wastes headroom.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add subsonic filtering or control low-frequency buildup.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Sub bass should be intentional and audible enough to manage.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use spectrum view plus kick, bass, and full-mix tests.

knowledgeCards.title: Step changes click
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Each step creates hard clicks or sharp edges.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Step smoothing shapes how abruptly sequenced values change.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Hard steps sound rhythmic. Smooth steps avoid clicks.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add short smoothing or attack time to step transitions.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hard clicks can be valid for glitch percussion if intentional.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Run sustained pads, bass, and noise through the sequencer.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep hard steps? It becomes a rhythmic artifact.

knowledgeCards.title: Sidechain input does nothing
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: External sidechain mode does not react to the key signal.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: External sidechain lets another track trigger the processor.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Sidechain routing is a contract with the host and user.
knowledgeCards.fix: Connect the external sidechain to the detector and expose clear status.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: A sidechain switch that does nothing should not ship.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kick-to-bass ducking or key-gated noise.

knowledgeCards.title: Sidechain listen plays the wrong signal
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The audition button does not play the signal controlling the processor.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Listen mode helps users hear what the detector is reacting to.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Monitoring does not change tone, but it teaches users how to tune the effect.
knowledgeCards.fix: Route listen mode to the actual detector input.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Wrong listen routing breaks learning and trust.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Toggle listen while changing sidechain filter or external input.

knowledgeCards.title: Spectral processing adds watery artifacts
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound gets watery, chirpy, or filled with tiny warbles.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Spectral smoothing controls how aggressively bins change over time.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More processing removes more problem sound. Less processing sounds natural.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce amount or increase smoothing/quality.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Watery artifacts can be a deliberate sci-fi texture.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, cymbals, room noise, and sustained notes.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the watery artifacts? It should be presented as character.

knowledgeCards.title: Spectral effect smears detail
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Transients and words lose definition and turn into a cloud.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Spectral windowing trades time detail for frequency detail.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More smear creates pads and washes. Less smear preserves articulation.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce blur/window size or blend in more dry signal.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Smearing is valid for ambient, drone, and frozen textures.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use vocals, drums, piano, and plucked instruments.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the smear? It turns clear audio into texture.

knowledgeCards.title: Bypass cuts off musical tails
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Echoes, reverb, or textures stop abruptly when bypass is touched.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Tail behavior decides whether time-based effects ring out after bypass.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Cutting tails is clean and instant. Spillover sounds smoother.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add tail spillover or fade tails on bypass.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hard tail cuts can be intentional for rhythmic mute effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Bypass during long reverb, delay, and freeze tails.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hard cut? It should feel like a performance mute.

knowledgeCards.title: Bypass still leaks effect
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin still changes the audio after bypass is enabled.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Bypass should clearly define whether audio is fully off or tail-only.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Tail spillover is musical. Unlabeled wet leakage is confusing.
knowledgeCards.fix: Mute wet processing on bypass or label tail spillover explicitly.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Bypass leakage should not be hidden.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Bypass during wet effects and compare to a true dry path.

knowledgeCards.title: Center image moves
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The vocal, kick, bass, or center image leans left or right while processing.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Stereo link decides whether both sides react together or independently.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Linked sounds stable. Independent can sound wider or more animated.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use linked mode for stable stereo bus behavior.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Independent movement can be intentional for colorful stereo compression.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen to center sounds while processing hard.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the moving center? It may pull the mix image around.

knowledgeCards.title: M/S image decodes wrong
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Center and sides feel swapped, hollow, or unstable.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: M/S routing converts left/right into middle and side information and back.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: M/S is powerful, but wrong decode breaks stereo trust.
knowledgeCards.fix: Correct encode/decode routing and polarity.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Broken M/S routing should not ship as a hidden behavior.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use center vocal, side ambience, and mono checks.

knowledgeCards.title: Widener delays one side too much
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The sound gets wide but loses punch or shifts to one side.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Haas widening delays one side to create width.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More delay feels wide. Too much harms mono and center focus.
knowledgeCards.fix: Reduce side delay or add mono-safe width limiting.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Haas width can be useful for special effects and headphones.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Check mono, headphones, and centered percussion.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the Haas width? It may weaken mono playback.

knowledgeCards.title: Stutter slices click
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Looped slices create ticks at the repeat point.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Slice windows and crossfades smooth cut boundaries.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Sharp slices are punchy. Crossfades are cleaner.
knowledgeCards.fix: Add micro-crossfades at slice boundaries.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Clicks can be a glitch style if the user chose it.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sustained audio and rhythmic loops at several slice lengths.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the slice clicks? It should be an obvious glitch aesthetic.

knowledgeCards.title: Sub generator adds too much bass
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The low end gets huge but loose, boomy, or speaker-risky.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Subharmonic generation adds lower notes below the source.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More sub feels powerful. Too much steals headroom and muddies playback.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower sub amount, add a limiter, or restrict it to bass-friendly presets.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Big sub is valid for 808, bass, and sound-system effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kick, bass, club loops, and small-speaker checks.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the sub weight? It needs safe headroom.

knowledgeCards.title: Tape hiss is too loud
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Hiss becomes obvious during quiet sections or after playback stops.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Noise level controls the modeled machine hiss mixed into the signal.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Hiss adds realism. Too much distracts and raises the noise floor.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower default hiss or add a noise level control.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Hiss can be part of cassette, VHS, and sample-pack character.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Listen during silence, fades, and sparse vocals.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the hiss? It should feel like texture, not a defect.

knowledgeCards.title: Tape pitch drifts too much
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Sustained notes wobble, swim, or feel seasick.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Wow and flutter model slow and fast tape-speed movement.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More drift feels old and unstable. Less drift keeps pitch usable.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower wow/flutter or make the default more stable.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Heavy drift is valid for lo-fi, VHS, and psychedelic effects.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use piano, pad chords, vocals, and sustained guitar.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the drift? It turns pitch instability into the sound.

knowledgeCards.title: Tape low end blooms too much
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Kick, bass, and low notes get oversized or cloudy.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Head bump models the low-frequency rise that tape machines can add.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More bump feels warm and vintage. Too much eats headroom and muddies the mix.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower head bump or add a low trim after tape.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Big tape low end can be intentional for dub, hip-hop, and vintage drum sounds.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kick, bass, toms, and full mix loops.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the big tape bloom? It will make the low end larger by design.

knowledgeCards.title: Transient attack is overhyped
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Hits get clicky, pokey, or unnatural.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Attack shaping boosts or reduces the front edge of sounds.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More attack adds punch. Too much becomes click and harshness.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower attack amount or soften the transient shape.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme attack is useful for drum design and aggressive samples.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use kick, snare, percussion, and picked bass.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the extra punch? It may become clicky.

knowledgeCards.title: Sustain makes sounds ring
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Drums, rooms, or notes hang longer than expected.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Sustain shaping raises or lowers the body after the attack.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More sustain adds size. Less sustain tightens the groove.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower sustain or shorten the tail control.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Long sustain is useful for big drums and ambient hits.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use snare, toms, room mics, and acoustic guitar.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the long tail? It will fill more space.

knowledgeCards.title: User does not know what to listen for
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The creator sees a warning but does not know how to judge the change.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Audition guidance tells the user what audio source and listening target prove the fix.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: A repair UI should teach the ear, not only move controls.
knowledgeCards.fix: Attach a listening target to every fix and every keep decision.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Creative choices still need an audition target.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use source-specific examples like vocal S sounds, kick hits, bass notes, or stereo mono checks.

knowledgeCards.title: Control name is too technical
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The user cannot connect the control name to what they hear.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Labels and tooltips teach what the plugin is doing.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Technical names are precise. Music names are easier to use.
knowledgeCards.fix: Use music-first names with technical detail hidden in an advanced drawer.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Technical labels are okay in advanced mode, not the main musician flow.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Ask whether a non-engineer can predict what the knob changes.

knowledgeCards.title: Default knob position is misleading
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The plugin opens in a more extreme state than the knobs visually suggest.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Default positions teach users what the normal starting sound is.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Bold defaults show personality. Neutral defaults build trust.
knowledgeCards.fix: Set defaults so first sound matches visual expectation.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Extreme defaults can define a signature effect plugin.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Load the plugin fresh and listen before touching anything.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the bold default? It may surprise users.

knowledgeCards.title: Creative keep needs a lesson
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: The creator may keep a risky sound without understanding the tradeoff.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: The keep path records artistic intent after teaching the sonic consequence.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Freedom matters, but informed freedom is the product goal.
knowledgeCards.fix: Before accepting a keep, show what the user is choosing in plain musical terms.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: The behavior can be kept only after the lesson is acknowledged.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use the audition target tied to that issue.

knowledgeCards.title: Knob name does not match sound
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Turning a knob changes something different than the label suggests.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Control labels teach the user what they are shaping.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: Creative controls can be abstract, but they still need predictable meaning.
knowledgeCards.fix: Rename the control or remap it so the label matches the audible behavior.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Abstract names like Vibe or Smash are acceptable if the behavior is intentionally broad.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Turn the knob slowly and describe what actually changes.

knowledgeCards.title: Vinyl crackle distracts
knowledgeCards.whatYouHear: Clicks and crackles pull attention away from the music.
knowledgeCards.whatThisChanges: Crackle level controls how much record-surface noise is added.
knowledgeCards.tradeoff: More crackle adds nostalgia. Less crackle keeps the source clear.
knowledgeCards.fix: Lower crackle amount or make it preset-specific.
knowledgeCards.creativeKeep: Crackle is valid for lo-fi beats, intros, and texture presets.
knowledgeCards.listenFor: Use sparse piano, vocals, drums, and silence.
knowledgeCards.keepConfirmCopy: Keep the crackle? It becomes part of the arrangement.
